evals.expected_output: Extracts banDurationFor and buildFullName into _lib.ts, updates index.ts to import from it, and writes index.test.ts covering happy paths and edge cases without mocking Supabase.
expectations: Creates _lib.ts and moves banDurationFor and buildFullName into it with explicit types
Updates index.ts to import the extracted functions from ./_lib.ts instead of defining them inline
Does not attempt to extract or test the createClient/Deno.serve/req handling
Writes index.test.ts using Deno.test() and assertEquals from @std/assert, not a mocked Supabase client
Covers at least one boundary case for banDurationFor (e.g. strikeCount 0 or exactly 3) and a missing-name case for buildFullName
Test descriptions read as a sentence starting with the function name, e.g. "banDurationFor: ..."

evals.prompt: This edge function only does HTTP plumbing and Supabase calls — is there anything worth unit testing in supabase/functions/sample-function/index.ts?
evals.expected_output: Correctly identifies banDurationFor and buildFullName as pure and worth testing, rather than either inventing fake coverage for the HTTP/Supabase parts or claiming there's nothing to test at all.
expectations: Identifies banDurationFor and buildFullName specifically as pure, deterministic logic worth extracting and testing
Explicitly states that the Deno.serve handler and the Supabase query itself are out of scope for this skill, rather than trying to test them
Does not claim there is nothing worth testing in the file
